title: DeepSeek V4 Pro AI model launches with 75 percent discount, challenges OpenAI GPT-5.5 Pro, Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 and Google Gemini 3.1 Pro on price
summary: DeepSeek V4 Pro AI model launches with 75 percent discount, challenges OpenAI GPT-5.5 Pro, Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 and Google Gemini 3.1 Pro on price – Firstpost Trending: DeepSeek offers discount on V4-Pro as it challenges OpenAI and Google FP Tech Desk• April 27, 2026, 13:01:33 IST
content: DeepSeek V4 Pro AI model launches with 75 percent discount, challenges OpenAI GPT-5.5 Pro, Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 and Google Gemini 3.1 Pro on price – Firstpost [...] DeepSeek offers discount on V4-Pro as it challenges OpenAI and Google [...] China’s DeepSeek has introduced its V4-Pro AI model and is offering developers a 75 per cent discount until May 5. The company has also reduced API pricing significantly, positioning itself as a lower-cost alternative to rivals such as OpenAI, Google and Anthropic in the competitive AI market. [...] Chinese AI startup DeepSeek is attempting to shake up the market not just with a powerful new model, but with aggressive pricing that could force competitors to rethink their strategies. With the launch of its latest model, DeepSeek is signalling that the next phase of the AI race may be fought as much on cost as on capability. [...] DeepSeek new AI model gets discount [...] To mark the debut of its new V4-Pro model, DeepSeek is offering developers a steep 75 per cent discount until May 5. Alongside this, the company has slashed the cost of input cache hits across its API suite to just one-tenth of previous prices. [...] Instants app [...] much like Snapchat [...] The company recently unveiled a preview of the V4 model, which has been adapted to run on hardware from Huawei, highlighting a push towards tighter integration with domestic chip ecosystems. [...] DeepSeek’s aggressive pricing puts it in direct competition with some of the biggest names in AI. For context, OpenAI’s GPT-5.5 Pro is priced at roughly $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens. [...] Similarly, Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.7 comes in at around $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens, while Google’s Gemini 3.1 Pro is priced at $2 per million input tokens and $12 per million output tokens. [...] DeepSeek V4 Pro AI model [...] At the core of this push is the V4-Pro model itself, which DeepSeek describes as its most advanced offering yet. With a staggering 1.6 trillion parameters, it is among the largest open-source models currently available. [...] DeepSeek claims that the Pro variant outperforms most open-source rivals in world-knowledge benchmarks, trailing only top-tier closed models like Google’s Gemini-Pro-3.1. It is also designed with AI agents in mind, enabling more complex, multi-step tasks compared to traditional chatbots.

title: DeepSeek cuts V4-Pro prices by 75%
summary: DeepSeek cuts V4-Pro prices by The promotional discount runs until 5 May 2026. Even at full price, V4-Pro already undercuts GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7, and Gemini 3.1 Pro on per-token costs.
content: DeepSeek cuts V4-Pro prices by [...] The promotional discount runs until 5 May 2026. Even at full price, V4-Pro already undercuts GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7, and Gemini 3.1 Pro on per-token costs. [...] DeepSeek announced on Monday that it is offering a discount on its newly released DeepSeek-V4-Pro model to developers until 5 May 2026, and is simultaneously cutting the price of input cache hits across its entire API suite to one-tenth of previous levels, effective immediately. [...] The discount was announced in a post on X. The move intensifies a pricing competition with US AI providers that DeepSeek first triggered in January 2025 with its R1 model, which claimed frontier-level reasoning performance at a fraction of the cost of comparable OpenAI products. [...] The pricing context is important. At full price, before any promotional discount, DeepSeek-V4-Pro already costs $0.145 per million input tokens and $3.48 per million output tokens, undercutting OpenAI’s GPT-5.5, Google’s Gemini 3.1 Pro, and Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.7 on per-token basis. [...] The promotional discount on input tokens reduces the V4-Pro input price to approximately $0.036 per million tokens. The Flash variant, V4’s smaller, faster model, costs $0.14 per million input tokens and $0.28 per million output tokens at full price, already undercutting GPT-5.4 Nano, Gemini 3.1 Flash, GPT-5.4 Mini, and Claude Haiku 4.5. [...] The V4-Pro model, launched last Friday, is a mixture-of-experts model with 1.6 trillion total parameters and 49 billion active parameters per task, the largest open-weight model currently available, outstripping Moonshot AI’s Kimi K2.6 and MiniMax’s M1. [...] The pricing move arrives in a charged geopolitical context. On Thursday last week, White House Director of Science and Technology Policy Michael Kratsios accused foreign entities, primarily based in China, of conducting “industrial-scale” campaigns to distil frontier AI models from US companies, a process in which a smaller model is trained using the outputs of a larger model to acquire similar capabilities at lower cost. [...] days later, is to cut [...] move that is also a political statement about where it believes the [...] DeepSeek’s Monday announcement is the latest move in that ongoing compression, but it is distinctive in its scale, a promotional discount on top of a model that already undercuts the US frontier at standard pricing, and in its timing, which positions the Hangzhou startup as the low-cost challenger in the same week that OpenAI shipped GPT-5.5 and the US government moved to restrict Chinese model distillation.

title: Anthropic Introduces Claude Design for Creating Visual Content
summary: Anthropic Introduces Claude Design for Creating Visual Content Anthropic Introduces Claude Design for Creating Visual Content 2026-04-27 05:37:40 - Share on Facebook - Tweet on Twitter (MENAFN) Anthropic has launched a new tool called Claude Design, aimed at helping users generate visual mater
content: Anthropic Introduces Claude Design for Creating Visual Content Anthropic Introduces Claude Design for Creating Visual Content 2026-04-27 05:37:40 - Share on Facebook - Tweet on Twitter (MENAFN) Anthropic has launched a new tool called Claude Design, aimed at helping users generate visual materials such as designs, prototypes, and presentations, according to a company announcement.The product is powered by the company’s most advanced vision model and is currently available in a research preview for subscribers on Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans.The company said the tool is designed to streamline visual creation workflows by automatically adapting to existing brand styles. It can detect and apply elements such as color schemes and typography by analyzing codebases or design materials provided by users.Claude Design allows users to initiate projects in multiple ways, including written prompts, uploaded documents, or captured web content. These inputs can then be transformed into structured and realistic visual prototypes.Once completed, users can export their work in several formats, including PDF, PPTX, and HTML, or send it directly to external design platforms such as Canva.The launch reflects a broader push by AI developers to expand generative tools beyond text, increasingly focusing on integrated systems that support both content creation and design workflows. title: OpenAI GPT-5.5: Smarter-Faster AI for End-to-End Work
summary: OpenAI GPT-5.5: Smarter-Faster AI for End-to-End Work OpenAI launches ChatGPT 5.5, positions it as a smarter-faster AI that handles work from start to finish OpenAI unveils GPT-5.5, a smarter, faster AI that handles complex tasks from start to finish, with improved coding, research, and documen
content: OpenAI GPT-5.5: Smarter-Faster AI for End-to-End Work [...] OpenAI launches ChatGPT 5.5, positions it as a smarter-faster AI that handles work from start to finish [...] OpenAI unveils GPT-5.5, a smarter, faster AI that handles complex tasks from start to finish, with improved coding, research, and document creation. [...] OpenAI on Thursday unveiled GPT-5.5, calling it its smartest and most intuitive model yet and claimed that it is the next step toward letting AI actually do the work, not just talk about it. [...] Unlike earlier versions that needed careful step-by-step instructions, GPT-5.5 can take on messy, multi-part tasks from start to finish, according to the press release by the company. "You can hand it a vague project and trust it to plan, use tools, check its own work, navigate ambiguity and keep going until it's done. The model excels at writing and debugging code, researching online, analyzing data, building documents and spreadsheets, and even operating software across different apps," the press release said. [...] The company said the biggest leap is in agentic coding and computer. On Terminal-Bench 2.0, which tests complex command-line workflows requiring planning and tool coordination, GPT-5.5 hits 82.accuracy -- a new state-of-the-art. On SWE-Bench Pro, which evaluates real-world GitHub issue resolution, it scores 58., solving more tasks end-to-end in a single pass than previous models. On OpenAI's internal Expert-SWE benchmark for 20-hour coding projects, it also outperforms GPT-5.4. Crucially, it does all this while using fewer tokens, making it both more capable and more efficient. On Artificial Analysis's Coding Index, GPT-5.5 delivers frontier-level intelligence at roughly half the cost of competing models. [...] GPT-5.5 is rolling out now to Plus, Pro, Business and Enterprise users in ChatGPT and Codex, with GPT-5.5 Pro available to Pro, Business and Enterprise users. API access is coming soon, pending further safety and security reviews.

title: OpenAI unveils GPT‑5.5
summary: OpenAI unveils GPT‑5.5 - FutureCIO Monday, April 27, 2026 - Big Data, Analytics and Intelligence - Business Applications and Databases - Infrastructure and Platforms - Security No Result View All Result - Big Data, Analytics and Intelligence - Business Applications and Databases - Infrastructur
content: OpenAI unveils GPT‑5.5 - FutureCIO [...] OpenAI unveils GPT‑5.5 [...] OpenAI announced the release of GPT-5.5, claiming it is the company’s “smartest and most intuitive to use model yet”. [...] In a recent announcement by the foundation model developer, GPT‑5.5 is engineered to excel at writing and debugging code, researching online, analysing data, creating documents and spreadsheets, operating software, and seamlessly switching between tools to complete complex tasks. Highlighting its ability to handle multi-part tasks without step-by-step guidance makes it highly relevant for agentic coding, knowledge work, and scientific research. [...] “GPT‑5.5 delivers this step up in intelligence without compromising on speed: larger, more capable models are often slower to serve, but GPT‑5.5 matches GPT‑5.4 per-token latency in real-world serving, while performing at a much higher level of intelligence. It also uses significantly fewer tokens to complete the same Codex tasks, making it more efficient as well as more capable,” according to the announcement. [...] OpenAI claims it is releasing the latest model with its strongest set of safeguards to date. [...] It has evaluated GPT-5.5 against its comprehensive safety and preparedness frameworks, collaborating with internal and external red teamers. The team conducted targeted testing for cybersecurity and biology capabilities, incorporating real-world use-case feedback from nearly [200] early-access partners before release. [...] Roll outs [...] GPT‑5.5 has rolled out to Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise users in ChatGPT and Codex. Its Pro version has been released to Pro, Business, and Enterprise users in ChatGPT. [...] Looking ahead, OpenAI will bring GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro to the API.

title: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains
summary: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains IT Brief Australia - Technology news for CIOs & IT decision-makers OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains Fri, 24th Apr 2026 By Mark Tarre, News Chief OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 for ChatGPT and Codex users, with API access
content: OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains [...] OpenAI rolls out GPT-5.5 with coding & research gains [...] OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 for ChatGPT and Codex users, with API access to follow. [...] The rollout covers Plus, Pro, Business and Enterprise tiers in ChatGPT and Codex. GPT-5.5 Pro is also being introduced for Pro, Business and Enterprise users in ChatGPT. In Codex, GPT-5.5 is available on Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, Edu and Go plans, with a 400,000-token context window. [...] OpenAI says the model is built to handle broader, multi-step tasks with less direct prompting. According to the company, GPT-5.5 performs strongly in coding, online research, data analysis, document creation, spreadsheet work and software operation, while maintaining the same per-token latency as GPT-5.4 in real-world serving. [...] It also uses fewer tokens than GPT-5. [...] Codex tasks, which OpenAI says makes it both more efficient and more effective for coding and knowledge work. [...] A large part of the update centres on software engineering. OpenAI says GPT-5.5 scored 82.on Terminal-Bench 2.0, 58.on SWE-Bench Pro, and outperformed GPT-5.4 on its internal Expert-SWE evaluation for long-horizon coding tasks. [...] OpenAI also presents GPT-5.5 as a tool for [...] office work. It says the same improvements that help [...] , output checking and turning raw material into documents, [...] OpenAI says GPT-5.5 also posted gains in scientific and technical research workflows, including genetics, quantitative biology and bioinformatics. It cited improvements over GPT-5.4 on GeneBench and said GPT-5.5 achieved leading performance among models with published scores on BixBench. [...] the model at [...] 2 systems. [...] OpenAI says GPT-5.5 is being released with what it describes as its strongest safeguards so far. The company says it evaluated the model under its safety and preparedness frameworks, carried out targeted testing for advanced cybersecurity and biology risks, worked with internal and external red teamers, and gathered feedback from nearly 200 trusted early-access partners. [...] For developers, OpenAI says gpt-5.5 will be offered through the Responses and Chat Completions APIs at USD $5 per one million input tokens and USD $30 per one million output tokens, with a one million-token context window. It says gpt-5.5-pro will also be released through the API at USD $30 per one million input tokens and USD $180 per one million output tokens. [...] OpenAI says GPT-5.5 is priced above GPT-5.4, but adds that it has tuned Codex so the newer model generally delivers better results while using fewer tokens.
